# MVT Games - llms.txt

> MVT (Model-View-Ticker) is an architecture for frame-based interactive
> applications. This file provides a compressed entry point for LLMs.

## Architecture

MVT splits applications into three layers:

- **Model** - owns state and domain logic. Advances only via update(deltaMs).
  No wall-clock time, no view references, no presentation values.
- **View** - reads state, writes presentation output. Stateless (no domain
  state). refresh() runs each frame, must be idempotent and side-effect free.
- **Ticker** - drives the frame loop: model.update(deltaMs) then
  view.update(deltaMs) (views with state) then view.refresh() then render.
- **Bindings** - contract between view and model. get*() reads state, on*()
  relays user input. Reusable views use bindings; top-level views access
  models directly.

## Frame Sequence

Every frame: compute deltaMs, cap it, update models, advance view
presentation state, refresh views, render. Models settle before views read.

## Key Rules

- Models: all state through update(deltaMs) only. No setTimeout, Date.now(),
  auto-playing tweens. Domain-level coordinates (tiles, world-units), not
  pixels. Parent delegates update() to children.
- Views: no domain state. refresh() is idempotent, read-only. Re-read
  bindings every frame (never cache at construction). May hold cosmetic
  presentation state (gains update(deltaMs)). Extract complex presentation
  logic to a view model.
- Ticker: strict sequence (update, refresh, render). Cap deltaMs. No domain
  logic or rendering code.
- Hot paths: no per-tick allocations. Index-based for loops. Pre-allocated
  structures. Change detection for expensive infrequent rebuilds.

## Project Conventions (this repo only)

- Factory functions, not classes: createXxxModel(options) -> XxxModel
- Barrel imports only: never import past index.ts
- String-literal unions, not enums
- 4-space indent, lower-kebab-case files, PascalCase types, camelCase rest
- TypeScript 5.9, Pixi.js 8, GSAP 3, Vite 7

## Documentation

- docs/architecture/ - transferable MVT specification (language-neutral)
- docs/learn/ - sequential introduction (5 pages)
- docs/topics/ - in-depth topic pages (time, composition, testing, etc.)
- docs/reference/ - rules, style guide, glossary, project structure
- docs/ai-agents/ - agent orientation and task-specific skills files
- AGENTS.md - compressed agent entry point (canonical)
